pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.not_connected: No Flow: not connected to a network
pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.no_flow_dry: No Flow: pipe is dry, nothing reaching it
pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.no_flow_starve: No Flow: pump can't pull its supply (check the source)
pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.no_flow_settle: No Flow: settled, levels are balanced
pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.blocked: No Flow: this line is shut
pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.stalled: No Flow: the tank or machine ahead is full
pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.no_head: No Flow: not enough RPM to lift this high
pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.reach_limit: Reach limit: raise the supply or add a pump
pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.detail.valve: No Flow: a valve or filter is shut here
pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.detail.pump_of: No Flow: a pump on this line is unpowered
pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.detail.crest: No Flow: air break over the crest
pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.detail.sink_fu: No Flow: the destination is full
pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.detail.source_: No Flow: the supply can't be drawn
pipesnphysics.ponder.tag.pipe_physics.de: Realistic fluid pressure, lift limits, and flow for Create pipe networks
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_1: A fluid tank filled with water
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_2: We want to move the fluid to this empty tank
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_4: Fluid won't move on its own through horizontal pipes — it needs a pump
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_5: A Mechanical Pump adds head to push fluid through the network
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_6: Give it rotational power and the pump starts moving fluid
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_7: Throughput and lift both depend on RPM — inspect with Engineer's Goggles
pipesnphysics.ponder.friction.text_2: Throughput falls as the run grows longer
pipesnphysics.ponder.friction.text_3: Viscous fluids like lava are hit even harder on the same layout
pipesnphysics.ponder.friction.text_4: Gravity assists on downhill runs — the pump gets help instead of fighting lift
pipesnphysics.ponder.friction.text_5: Sneak with goggles on a running pump to see lift vs pipe-run limits
pipesnphysics.ponder.pressure.text_2: Fluid naturally seeks the lower tank
pipesnphysics.ponder.pressure.text_3: Connected tanks equalize to the same surface height
pipesnphysics.ponder.pressure.text_4: A mechanical pump adds head from RPM when it must push uphill
pipesnphysics.ponder.pressure.text_5: Use Engineer's Goggles to read flow, pressure, and why a line stalled
pipesnphysics.ponder.l_shape.text_1: A high tank builds head down the vertical leg of the run
pipesnphysics.ponder.l_shape.text_2: That head is preserved on the vertical drop
pipesnphysics.ponder.l_shape.text_3: The horizontal leg spends head on friction — only so much reach remains
pipesnphysics.ponder.l_shape.text_4: More height above the turn means more horizontal reach
pipesnphysics.ponder.uphill.text_1: A tank at ground level feeding a vertical run
pipesnphysics.ponder.uphill.text_2: The pump must supply enough head for every block of rise
pipesnphysics.ponder.uphill.text_3: Default 0.25 blocks of head per RPM — 64 RPM lifts about 16 blocks
pipesnphysics.ponder.uphill.text_4: Stack pumps in series or raise RPM to reach higher tanks
pipesnphysics.ponder.vertical_drop.text_: No pump is required for a simple drop between two tanks
The upper tank drains until both surfaces match — fluid is conserved every tick
pipesnphysics.ponder.siphon.text_1: Pulling over a tall crest creates suction at the highest pipe
pipesnphysics.ponder.siphon.text_2: If the column hangs too far below that point, the siphon breaks
pipesnphysics.ponder.siphon.text_3: Goggles will report an 'air break' when the suction limit is exceeded
pipesnphysics.ponder.goggles.text_1: Wear goggles and look at any pipe or pump in a live network
pipesnphysics.ponder.goggles.text_2: Read flow, head remaining, stall reasons, and pump load while sneaking
